economic_hypothesis: When independently constructed ES volume and dollar event clocks close in the same availability minute, agreement in aggressive-flow direction can reveal inventory pressure that liquidity providers have not yet fully repriced; disagreement can reveal absorbed or exhausted pressure.
who_pays: Short-horizon liquidity suppliers and late aggressive followers whose inventory adjustment lags cross-clock confirmation.
persistence_rationale: Volume and dollar clocks partition the same prints by different stopping rules, so agreement is a cross-representation confirmation rather than a threshold neighbor; execution remains slower than HFT and uses the following minute open.

source.availability_alignment: floor(availability_ns / 60_seconds) equal for the last completed bar of each required type

motifs.definition: nonzero signed_aggressor_volume has the same sign on the last completed VOLUME_BAR and DOLLAR_BAR in the availability minute
flow signs agree and each bar's price_change_points has the same nonzero sign as its own aggressive flow
nonzero signed_aggressor_volume signs are opposite across the last completed VOLUME_BAR and DOLLAR_BAR

signal_policy.decision_timestamp: maximum availability_ns of the aligned completed pair
signal_policy.entry: first explicit-contract 1m open at or after decision timestamp
signal_policy.exit: explicit-contract 1m open at entry plus frozen holding_minutes

CONTRE: Both clocks are derived from the same underlying prints, so apparent confirmation can still be duplicated information or account geometry; novelty deduplication and the permanent tripwire are mandatory.
